title: Backup, Point-in-Time Recovery, Rebuild, and Disaster Reconciliation · Execution Trace: Feed Go Replay Order
summary: Backups exist but are untested, feed content is assumed refetchable, reader state shares no recovery objective, restore replays workers early, and corruption remains hidden. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go replay order increment in rehearsed feed-service recovery system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Define migration, database restore, secret rotation, scheduler pause, validator review, worker resume, cache rebuild, and client reopening order.
Restore into isolation, validate schema and application versions, reconcile counts and invariants, and prevent outbound fetching during inspection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Replay Order · Theory Feed Go Replay Order
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 8643 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove data-class objectives, backup eligibility, isolated restore, safe resume order, and disaster drills; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
